circular.title: Master Circular – Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
circular.summary: This Master Circular consolidates guidelines for Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) implementation, providing a framework for banks to assist Self Help Groups (SHGs) of rural poor, particularly women. It outlines credit linkage processes, loan amounts, repayment schedules, and security requirements. The mission aims at poverty reduction through building strong institutions for the poor and enabling access to financial services.

clauses.text: DAY-NRLM promotes affinity-based women SHGs. Groups for persons with disabilities, elders, and transgenders may include both men and women.
SHGs consist of 10-20 members; special SHGs (remote/tribal/disabled) may have a minimum of 5 members.
MoRD provides RF support of ₹20,000 - ₹30,000 per SHG to groups in existence for 3/6 months that follow 'Panchasutras'.
Banks shall open savings accounts for SHGs and their federations.
Banks are advised to put in place dual-authentication facility in both ON-US and OFF-US environments for BC-managed retail outlets.
SHGs must be in active existence for at least 6 months and practicing 'Panchasutras'.
Banks must use Common Loan Application Forms devised by IBA.
Banks are advised to sanction a minimum CCL of ₹6 lakh for 3 years.
Banks may consider loans up to ₹10 lakh to individual members of matured SHGs for viable enterprises.
MCPs prepared by SHGs form the basis for determining the purpose.
For loans up to ₹10.00 lakh, no collateral and no margin shall be obtained. No lien on savings accounts.
Willful defaulters should not be financed. Non-willful defaulters should not be debarred; genuine defaults should follow restructuring norms.
Loan passbooks in regional languages should be issued and updated with every transaction.
Banks shall prepare a list of defaulting SHGs every month and furnish it to BLBC and DCC meetings.
SLBCs shall constitute a sub-committee on SHG Bank Linkage.
Banks shall coordinate with SRLMs to utilize Financial Literacy Community Resource Persons (FL-CRPs) for village camps.
Banks may share data with DAY-NRLM/SRLMs in mutually agreed formats, ensuring specific and separate customer consent.

obligations.text: Adhere to 'Panchasutras' (regular meetings, savings, lending, recoveries, and books) for SHG eligibility.
obligations.plain_english: Follow the five norms of good SHGs

obligations.text: Opening of savings accounts for SHG members must not be a prerequisite for credit linkage.
obligations.plain_english: Do not require savings accounts for credit linkage

obligations.text: Maintain separate savings and loan accounts for SHGs.
Implement dual-authentication facility at BC retail outlets for SHG transactions.
Sanction minimum Cash Credit Limit (CCL) of ₹6 lakh to eligible SHGs for 3 years.
Provide repeat loans to eligible SHGs.
obligations.plain_english: Provide repeat loans to eligible SHGs

obligations.text: Provide minimum OD facility of ₹5000 to every woman SHG member with a PMJDY account.
Ensure no collateral or margin is obtained for loans to SHGs up to ₹10 lakh.
obligations.plain_english: Do not take collateral for loans up to ₹10 lakh

obligations.text: Ensure no collateral is obtained for loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹20 lakh; entire loan is CGFMU eligible.
obligations.plain_english: Do not take collateral for loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹20 lakh

obligations.text: Rejection of SC/ST loan applications under govt programmes must occur at the next higher level.
obligations.plain_english: Reject SC/ST loan applications at the next higher level

obligations.text: Set up a special cell at Head Office to monitor credit flow to SC/ST beneficiaries.
obligations.plain_english: Set up a special cell to monitor credit flow

obligations.text: Furnish state-wise consolidated progress reports to RBI quarterly.
obligations.plain_english: Submit quarterly progress reports to RBI

prohibitions.text: Willful defaulters should not be financed.
Non-willful defaulters should not be debarred; genuine defaults should follow restructuring norms.

definitions.definition: The five norms of good SHGs: regular meetings, regular savings, regular internal lending, regular recoveries, and maintenance of proper books of accounts.
Inclusive of revolving funds, own savings, interest earned from on-lending, and other income sources.
A document prepared by SHG members to prioritize and plan for credit requirements.
The limit up to which an SHG can withdraw funds from its Cash Credit account.
Transaction authenticated by two members of the SHG through Aadhaar and biometrics.

references.circular_number: RBI Master Direction on Treatment of Wilful Defaulters
Master Circular on Customer Service in Banks
